jobs.title: Junior Data Analyst
jobs.description:
Junior Data Analyst – Role Description & Qualifications
Role Overview
The Junior Data Analyst supports data-driven decision-making by collecting, processing, and analyzing data to generate meaningful insights. This role is designed for early-career professionals who are eager to develop analytical skills, work with structured datasets, and collaborate with cross-functional teams to support business objectives. The position offers hands-on experience with data analysis tools, reporting, and performance measurement.
Key Responsibilities
• Collect, clean, and validate data from multiple sources to ensure accuracy and consistency.
• Assist in analyzing datasets to identify trends, patterns, and performance indicators.
• Prepare regular and ad hoc reports, dashboards, and data summaries for internal stakeholders.
• Support data visualization efforts to present insights in a clear and actionable manner.
• Assist in maintaining databases and ensuring proper data documentation.
• Work closely with senior analysts to understand business requirements and analytical methodologies.
• Participate in quality checks and data governance activities.
• Help automate routine data processing and reporting tasks where applicable.
• Support performance tracking by monitoring key metrics and indicators.
• Contribute to continuous improvement of data analysis processes and tools.
Qualifications and Requirements
• Bachelor’s degree in Data Analytics, Statistics, Mathematics, Computer Science, Economics, or a related field.
• Basic understanding of data analysis concepts, statistical methods, and data structures.
• Familiarity with spreadsheets, databases, and data visualization tools.
• Introductory knowledge of query languages or analytical scripting is an advantage.
• Strong attention to detail and commitment to data accuracy.
• Analytical mindset with problem-solving capabilities.
• Ability to communicate findings clearly to both technical and non-technical audiences.
• Strong organizational skills and ability to manage multiple tasks.
• Willingness to learn new tools, techniques, and analytical frameworks.
• Experience from academic projects, internships, or personal data analysis work is a plus.
Summary
The Junior Data Analyst plays an important role in transforming raw data into valuable insights that support operational and strategic decisions. This position provides a solid foundation for building a career in data analytics through practical experience, collaboration, and continuous learning.

jobs.company: Institut National des Postes et Télécommunications
jobs.title: Junior Data Analyst
jobs.description:
Role Description
This role is for a Junior Data Analyst responsible for supporting data analysis, reporting, and insight generation that contribute to informed business decisions. In this position, you will work with data under guidance, helping transform raw information into structured insights that support operational and strategic objectives.
As a Junior Data Analyst, you will assist with collecting, cleaning, and organizing data from various sources to ensure accuracy and consistency. You will support routine analysis, help maintain datasets, and contribute to the preparation of reports and dashboards used by internal teams.
Key responsibilities include analyzing basic trends and performance metrics, identifying data inconsistencies, and supporting ongoing analytical projects. You will help document methodologies, assumptions, and findings to ensure transparency and repeatability.
You will collaborate with analysts, managers, and stakeholders to understand data requirements and support analytical workflows. Your role will involve responding to data requests, maintaining reporting schedules, and assisting with continuous improvements in data quality and processes.
This position requires a strong interest in data analysis, attention to detail, and a structured approach to problem-solving. You will be expected to follow best practices, validate results, and continuously develop analytical skills.
Clear communication is essential, as you will help explain data findings in a simple and structured manner. By supporting accurate analysis and reliable reporting, the Junior Data Analyst plays an important role in enabling data-driven decision-making across the organization.
Key Responsibilities
Collect, clean, and organize data from multiple sources
Support routine data analysis and reporting activities
Assist in preparing dashboards, reports, and summaries
Identify data quality issues and support data validation
Maintain organized datasets and documentation
Support performance tracking and metric analysis
Respond to data requests from internal teams
Document analysis methods and assumptions
Follow data standards and best practices
Assist with continuous improvement of data processes

Qualifications
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills
High attention to detail and data accuracy
Ability to work with spreadsheets and basic analysis tools
Interest in data analysis and reporting
Ability to organize and interpret information clearly
Clear written and verbal communication skills
Ability to manage tasks and deadlines effectively
Willingness to learn and develop analytical skills
Structured and reliable working style

jobs.title: Data Analyst
jobs.description:
Data Analyst – Role Description & Qualifications
Role Overview
The Data Analyst is responsible for transforming raw data into meaningful insights that support business decisions and operational efficiency. This role focuses on data collection, analysis, reporting, and visualization to identify trends, measure performance, and uncover opportunities for improvement. The Data Analyst works closely with stakeholders across departments to ensure data-driven strategies align with organizational goals.
Key Responsibilities
• Collect, process, and analyze data from multiple internal and external sources.
• Validate, clean, and structure datasets to ensure accuracy and consistency.
• Develop reports, dashboards, and visualizations to communicate insights effectively.
• Analyze trends, patterns, and key performance indicators to support decision-making.
• Collaborate with business teams to understand analytical requirements and objectives.
• Support ad hoc analysis requests and present findings to stakeholders.
• Assist in defining data standards, metrics, and documentation practices.
• Identify data quality issues and recommend corrective actions.
• Contribute to automation and optimization of data analysis and reporting processes.
• Stay informed about analytical tools, techniques, and best practices.
Qualifications and Requirements
• Bachelor’s degree in Data Analytics, Statistics, Mathematics, Computer Science, Economics, or a related field.
• Strong understanding of data analysis techniques, statistical concepts, and data structures.
• Experience working with datasets, databases, and reporting tools.
• Proficiency in spreadsheets and data visualization platforms; familiarity with query languages or analytical scripting is a plus.
• Strong analytical thinking and problem-solving skills.
• High attention to detail and commitment to data accuracy.
• Ability to translate complex data into clear, actionable insights.
• Strong communication and collaboration skills.
• Ability to manage multiple projects and meet deadlines effectively.
• Experience gained through professional roles, academic projects, or internships is advantageous.
Summary
The Data Analyst plays a vital role in enabling data-driven decision-making by delivering accurate analysis and actionable insights. This position offers opportunities to work with diverse datasets, collaborate across teams, and contribute directly to strategic and operational improvements within the organization.

Description

The candidate will work in a team environment on various Navy projects relating to Environmental, Emergency Response, and Air Operations GIS data. The candidate will create and review geospatial data and detailed reports, communicate directly with clients and subject matter experts to understand project requirements, and lead a dispersed team of GIS analysts to ensure project goals are met. The candidate will develop, analyze, and perform quality control checks on geospatial data and be responsible for ensuring deliverables are accurate and of high quality.

Qualifications

Education and Experience

Bachelor's degree and 5 or more years of experience; High School diploma and 8 or more years’ experience in lieu of degree may be considered.

Required Skills

At least five years’ experience working directly with Esri software.
Experience converting multiple data types (non-geospatial, CAD, etc.) into a standardized format.
Experience performing quality control checks on geospatial data using Esri software (RBJ files or Python).
Experience leading a team of analysts, working directly with Government customers, and having ownership of deliverables.
Attention to detail and good communication skills.

Target salary range $80,001 - $120,000. The estimate displayed represents the typical salary range for this position based on experience and other factors.

jobs.title: Physics Data Analyst - AI Trainer
jobs.description:
We are looking for an existing Physics Data Analyst to help train AI models. You will measure the progress of these AI chatbots, evaluate their logic, and solve problems to improve the quality of each model.

In this role, you will need to hold an expert level of physics. Other related fields include, but are not limited to: Physics and/or Applied Mathematics.

Advantages Of Contracting With Us

You'll be able to choose which projects you want to work on and when
You work on your own schedule, on your own computer, from the comfort of your own home
Projects are paid hourly starting at $40+ USD per hour, with bonus rates available on some projects

Responsibilities

Give AI chatbots complex physics problems and evaluate their outputs
Evaluate the quality produced by AI models for correctness and performance

Qualifications

Fluency in English (native or bilingual level)
Detail-oriented
Strong grasp of classical mechanics, E&M, thermodynamics, and basic quantum concepts
A current, in progress, or completed Master's and/or PhD is preferred but not required

Note

Payment is made via PayPal. We will never ask for any money from you. PayPal will handle any currency conversions from USD. Only applicants in the United States will be considered for this role. This is an independent contractor position.

#physics

jobs.title: Senior Data Analyst
jobs.description:
U.S. News & World Report is a multifaceted digital media company dedicated to helping citizens, consumers, business leaders and policy officials make important decisions in their lives. We publish independent reporting, rankings, data journalism and advice that has earned the trust of our readers and users for nearly 90 years. Our platforms on usnews.com include Education, Health, Money, Travel, Cars, News and 360 Reviews.

We reach millions of people monthly during moments when they are most in need of expert advice and motivated to act on that advice directly on our platforms. Our signature franchises include our “Best” series of consumer guides on colleges, graduate schools, hospitals, diets, cars, financial services and more. These guides provide an easy-to-digest list for consumers to better understand and compare when making their decisions. We continue to publish annual guides of the authoritative Best Colleges and Best Hospitals rankings on our website and in print. And our U.S. News Live flagship conferences highlight important national conversations including Healthcare of Tomorrow.

Your role in helping us shape the future:

U.S. News empowers everyone to thrive. As a data analyst on our Money team, you will collaborate closely with a cross-functional team to guide the development of products and services from conception to launch, and engage closely with our partnerships and editorial teams to help maximize success on both fronts.

The primary responsibilities of the Data Analyst are:

Providing tactical support to product managers through the entire development lifecycle, including assisting with technical specifications and QA.
Analyzing key metrics to understand product performance, including running A/B tests, predictive modeling, and dashboard development
Generate revenue yield analysis and recommendations on partner campaigns/budgets (including both paid media and on page placements across the US News experience)
Access and manipulate large external datasets to enable our editorial experts to publish unique data driven content pieces and advice

Are you up to the challenge?

Proven ability to quickly adapt to a rapidly changing environment while maintaining quality and adhering to deadlines
Develop and maintain positive working relationships stakeholders across multiple functions and teams
Effectively share and communicate data insights and analysis across different parts of the organization from engineers to executives

You should definitely have:

Bachelor’s degree in statistics, data science, economics, computer science, or any other relevant fields
Minimum 5 years’ experience coding with statistical software packages, preferably STATA
Expert level proficiency in SQL, R, SAS, Python or other data science programming languages
Ability to work with large datasets and summarize key insights
Data mining, analysis, and presentation skills
Proficiency using Microsoft Excel or Google Sheets to summarize and present data in a concise and easy to understand manner
Strong research skills and the ability to answer questions with internal and external data sources
Experience presenting analyses to non-technical audiences with simple explanations
Attention to detail, organization and time management are your strengths

It would be nice if you have:

Experience with reporting/tracking tools including Google Analytics
Up-to-date knowledge on Internet publishing and metrics, with an acute understanding of SEO

What it’s like to work with us:

Talent is our best asset!

We invest in people with passion and potential who understand U.S. News’ dedication to our consumers.
Entrepreneurial, mission-driven culture with core values of quality and integrity
Focus on fostering personal and professional growth
Competitive benefits including paid vacation time, medical, tuition reimbursement, and training
Collaborative Work Environment : Fun, diverse, inclusive and ambitious co-workers

Other Job Info:

These statements are intended to describe the general nature and level of work being performed by employees assigned to this job. This is not intended to be an exhaustive list of all responsibilities, duties, and skills required of employees assigned to this job.
U.S. News & World Report strongly encourages all employees to be fully vaccinated (including boosters).
Preference for New York or Washington, DC based applicants to work a hybrid schedule in those office locations. Remote applicants will be considered.
The anticipated base salary for this position is $65,000-$85,000. The actual base salary offered will depend on a variety of factors, including without limitation, the qualifications of the individual applicant for the position, years of relevant experience, level of education attained, certifications or other professional licenses held, interview performance, and if applicable, the location in which the applicant lives and/or from which they will be performing the job.

jobs.title: Staff Environmental Scientist - NPDES Permitting (Multiple Locations)
jobs.description:
Description

The Staff Environmental Scientist will support NPDES permitting, water quality studies, environmental assessments, and compliance efforts for various types of projects including petrochemical plants, power generation, renewable fuels, industrial sites, and water treatment facilities. This position supports the growth of the Environmental Services Global Practice based upon Burns & McDonnell's core values of safety, client satisfaction, commitment, dedication, integrity, and overall outstanding service.

Assigned Tasks May Include The Following

Apply environmental regulatory knowledge and experience to develop effective strategies and solutions to current and future technical and regulatory issues on projects.
Evaluate wastewater and stormwater discharges associated with industrial projects in the early stages of development and provide permitting approach and operational compliance strategies.
Evaluate potential sites for environmental issues that may be critical to project development.
Support development of NPDES permit applications and associated studies including reasonable potential analysis, antidegradation analysis, 316(b) assessments, ELG applicability reviews, mixing zone analysis, and water quality modeling (e.g., CORMIX, QUAL2K, WASP). 
Monitor state and federal regulatory developments (proposed and promulgated rule making, notices, etc.).
Prepare operational and construction Stormwater Pollution Prevention Plans (SWPPP) and SPCC Plans and conduct compliance inspections and audits.
Establish, build, and maintain relationships with internal Burns & McDonnell customers (in the engineering practices) and external clients.
All other duties as assigned.

Qualifications

Bachelor's degree in Environmental Science, Chemistry, Toxicology, or related field from an accredited program is required.
Minimum of 4 years of related professional experience (5 years preferred). 
Prior consulting experience preferred.
Knowledge of federal and state regulations relating to water quality, e.g., Section 402 of the Clean Water Act.
Experience preparing NPDES permit applications and performing reasonable potential analyses.
Experience with large data sets. Laboratory experience preferred.
Regulatory and technical familiarity with wastewater and stormwater systems.
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills.
Excellent written & verbal communication skills.
High level of attention to detail.
Ability to adjust to rapidly changing work environments and research approaches.
Must be proficient in the use of computer software (Microsoft Word, Excel, PowerPoint).
Valid driver's license required. In addition, must meet standards to qualify for and maintain the Company's vehicle driving privileges as outlined in the Company's Motor Vehicle Safety Policy. 

This job posting will remain open a minimum of 72 hours and on an ongoing basis until filled.

EEO/Disabled/Veterans

jobs.title: Project Coordinator - Life Science Construction
jobs.description:
From the inception of a project through to completion and beyond, Turner & Townsend help to deliver the outcomes that matter through transformational programs covering the full spectrum of consultancy, project delivery and post-project operations.

With offices located globally, you're never far away from our services. Working from 133 offices in 49 countries, we make the difference to projects across the real estate, infrastructure and natural resources sectors worldwide.

Our team is dynamic, innovative and client-focused, supported by an inclusive and fun company culture. Our clients value our proactive approach, depth of expertise, integrity and the quality we deliver. As a result, our people get to enjoy working on some of the most exciting projects in the world.

Job Description

Turner & Townsend
are engaged to support our client with many complex projects, and we are seeking an ambitious Project Coordinator
to join our prestigious client’s rapidly growing Life Sciences team. You have a chance to be a key player in our technological future, as part of a team of innovative professionals shaping our built environment.

The role requires a smart, motivated professional to work closely with global facilities teams and various internal departments including Finance, Procurement, Security and Legal to support all aspects of their rapidly expanding footprint.
Assist in monitoring and documenting project progress and other key performance indicators.
Assist in managing internal project budgets within appropriate software platforms for monthly/quarterly/yearly financial reporting.
Assist in compiling contractual documentation to support contract and PO issuances for assigned vendors.
Perform document control duties as required.
Issue and track purchase orders for new and existing vendors.
Manage monthly vendor invoice review process within invoicing software, including project management review, 3rd party auditing services, and communication with Accounts Payable.
Facilitate change order routing and review processes within project management software.
Assist in scheduling coordination meetings and job walks.
Assist in financial closeout of internal budgets, Vendor POs, etc.

Qualifications

BA/BS or equivalent in financial management or controls. Relevant work experience will also be considered in lieu of degree.
Minimum of (3) year’s professional experience as project coordinator or administration.
Strong collaboration and relationship building skills.
Excellent communication skills and presentation skills.
A problem-solving mindset.
Expert in Microsoft and Google Suite of software products; especially Excel.

Additional Information

*On-site presence and requirements may change depending on our client's needs*

Our inspired people share our vision and mission. We provide a great place to work, where each person has the opportunity and voice to affect change.

We want our people to succeed both in work and life. To support this we promote a healthy, productive and flexible working environment that respects work-life balance.

Turner & Townsend is an equal opportunity employer. We celebrate diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees and actively encourage applications from all sectors of the community.

Please find out more about us at www.turnerandtownsend.com/ and https://www.heery.com/

All your information will be kept confidential according to EEO guidelines.

Join our social media conversations for more information about Turner & Townsend and our exciting future projects:

Twitter

Instagram

LinkedIn

It is strictly against Turner & Townsend policy for candidates to pay any fee in relation to our recruitment process. No recruitment agency working with Turner & Townsend will ask candidates to pay a fee at any time.

Any unsolicited resumes/CVs submitted through our website or to Turner & Townsend personal e-mail accounts, are considered property of Turner & Townsend and are not subject to payment of agency fees. In order to be an authorised Recruitment Agency/Search Firm for Turner & Townsend, there must be a formal written agreement in place and the agency must be invited, by the Recruitment Team, to submit candidates for review.

jobs.title: Data Analyst
jobs.description:
About The Company

Ryder System, Inc. is a leading provider of transportation and supply chain management solutions. With decades of experience, Ryder has established itself as a trusted partner for organizations seeking innovative logistics, fleet management, and dedicated transportation services. Committed to operational excellence and customer satisfaction, Ryder leverages advanced technology and industry expertise to deliver tailored solutions that drive efficiency and growth for its clients worldwide. The company's dedication to sustainability, safety, and continuous improvement underscores its position as a forward-thinking leader in the logistics industry.

About The Role

We are seeking a highly motivated and detail-oriented professional to join Ryder System, Inc. as a Supply Chain Analyst. In this role, you will be responsible for analyzing supply chain processes, identifying areas for improvement, and implementing strategic initiatives to optimize logistics operations. The ideal candidate will possess strong analytical skills, a deep understanding of supply chain management principles, and the ability to collaborate effectively with cross-functional teams. This position offers an excellent opportunity to contribute to the company's mission of delivering innovative solutions that enhance operational efficiency and customer satisfaction.

Qualifications

The ideal candidate should have a bachelor’s degree in Supply Chain Management, Logistics, Business Administration, or a related field. Proven experience of at least 3 years in supply chain analysis or logistics operations is preferred. Strong proficiency in data analysis tools such as Excel, SAP, or other ERP systems is essential. Excellent communication and problem-solving skills are required to effectively collaborate with team members and stakeholders. A solid understanding of transportation, warehousing, inventory management, and procurement processes is highly desirable. The ability to work under pressure and manage multiple priorities in a fast-paced environment is also important.

Responsibilities

Analyze supply chain data to identify inefficiencies and develop actionable insights for process improvement. Collaborate with logistics, procurement, and warehouse teams to streamline operations and reduce costs. Monitor key performance indicators (KPIs) and prepare detailed reports to track progress and inform decision-making. Assist in the development and implementation of supply chain strategies aligned with company objectives. Support the integration of new technologies and systems to enhance supply chain visibility and automation. Maintain accurate records of supply chain activities and ensure compliance with company policies and industry regulations. Participate in cross-functional projects aimed at optimizing transportation routes, inventory levels, and supplier relationships. Provide training and support to team members on best practices and new processes.

Benefits

Ryder offers a comprehensive benefits package that includes competitive salary, health insurance, dental and vision coverage, and retirement plans. Employees are also eligible for performance-based bonuses and incentives. The company promotes a healthy work-life balance through flexible work arrangements and paid time off. Additional benefits include professional development opportunities, tuition reimbursement, and access to wellness programs. Ryder’s commitment to employee growth and well-being makes it an ideal place for professionals seeking a rewarding career in supply chain management.

Equal Opportunity

Ryder System, Inc. is an equal opportunity employer. We celebrate diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees. We do not discriminate based on race, color, religion, gender, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, age, disability, or any other protected characteristic. We believe that a diverse workforce enhances our ability to serve our clients and innovate in the logistics industry, and we encourage individuals from all backgrounds to apply.

jobs.title: Health Data Analyst - AI Trainer
jobs.description:
About The Job

We are looking for existing quantitative professionals to help train AI models. In this role, you will work closely with state-of-the-art AI models on tasks like evaluating AI-generated quantitative analyses, solving technical problems, and providing feedback that directly shapes how these systems reason about data, models, and scientific problems. Whether your background is in data science, astrophysics, economics, biostatistics, operations research, or any other quantitative field, if you think rigorously about data and models, your skills are directly applicable here. Some team members fit this work alongside a full-time role, while others treat it as their primary focus.

To get started, once you sign up for an account, you'll take a short assessment (this serves as our version of an interview). If you pass, you'll receive an email confirmation, and paid work will become available on our platform.

Advantages Of Contracting With Us

You'll be able to choose which projects you want to work on and when
You work on your own schedule, on your own computer, from the comfort of your own home
Projects are paid hourly starting at $50-$100+ USD per hour, with bonus rates available on some projects

Responsibilities

Evaluate AI-generated quantitative work, including statistical analysis, predictive modeling, scientific reasoning, and data-driven insights, for technical accuracy and real-world validity.
Design and solve quantitative problems used to train and benchmark AI systems, spanning areas like forecasting, experimental analysis, optimization, and statistical inference.
Write clear technical explanations and well-documented analytical code.
Provide feedback that directly shapes the next generation of AI models built for quantitative reasoning.

Qualifications

2+ years of hands-on experience in a quantitative role or research environment — such as data science, statistics, economics, finance, physics, biology, epidemiology, operations research, or any adjacent field.
Some coding experience required, with comfort writing and reviewing analytical code end-to-end.
Practical experience with statistical methods, predictive modeling, and experiment design (e.g., A/B testing, hypothesis testing, regression, classification, time-series forecasting).
Fluency in English (native or bilingual level) with strong writing skills.
A bachelor's degree in a quantitative field is preferred (Statistics, Computer Science, Mathematics, Engineering, or similar); a master's or PhD is a plus.
Relevant credentials are a plus (e.g., Kaggle Competition ranking, AWS/GCP ML certifications, or equivalent demonstrated expertise).

Note: Payment is made via PayPal. We will never ask for any money from you. PayPal will handle any currency conversions from USD. Only applicants in the United States, Canada, United Kingdom, Ireland, Australia, and New Zealand will be considered for this role. This is an independent contractor position.

jobs.title: Support Operations Data Analyst
jobs.description:
Swooped is a Talent Platform (not a staffing agency). We are not the employer for this role and do not make hiring decisions. Clicking "Apply" takes you to Swooped to review the role and hiring company details.

About the Opportunity

The organization is transforming how legal and professional services operate. By combining frontier agentic AI, an enterprise-grade platform, and deep domain expertise, the company is reshaping how critical knowledge work gets done for decades to come.

This is a rare chance to help build a generational company at a true inflection point. With a significant customer base in numerous countries, strong product-market fit, and world-class investor support, the organization is scaling fast and defining a new category in real time. The work is ambitious, the bar is high, and the opportunity for growth — personal, professional, and financial — is unmatched.

The team moves fast, takes ownership, and is deeply committed to the mission — operating with intensity, staying close to customers, and pushing each other for excellence. The organization lives by three values: Decisiveness, Simplicity, and Job's Not Finished. Actions are taken quickly on clear judgment over perfect information, simplicity is believed to be what scales, and there is never satisfaction with the current state. If you want to do the best work of your career alongside people who share that drive, the organization would love to build with you.

The future of professional services is being written today — and the organization is just getting started.

Role Overview

User Operations runs on data — but right now, that data lives in too many places, speaks too many languages, and reaches the wrong people too late. This role exists to fix that.

As the first Support Operations Data Analyst
, this role will own the analytics function for the User Operations organization. The individual will build and maintain the dashboards, reports, and feedback loops that indicate whether the organization is hitting its north stars — cSAT, TTR, QA scores, escalation rates — and surface the signal underneath the numbers so action can be taken. This role will sit within the Support Operations team, reporting to the Support Operations Manager, and work closely with User Operations leadership and the central data team to ensure the organization is equipped with the right instrumentation as it scales.

This is a solo role. There will not be a team beneath the individual. The candidate will need to be fluent enough in support analytics to hold the function independently, confident enough to push back on how metrics are framed, and fast enough to operate at the organization's pace.

Key Responsibilities

Own recurring reporting for User Operations — weekly, monthly, and QBR-ready — tailored to ops, leadership, and cross-functional audiences
Translate support data into clear narratives: what's happening, why, and what to do about it
Track and maintain north star metrics: cSAT, TTR by tier, QA scores, bug escalation rate to EPD, and First Response Time
Build and maintain self-serve dashboards that give the ops team and leadership real-time visibility into support performance
Partner with Support Systems to ensure Zendesk is instrumented to capture the data needed
Work with the central data team to connect support data to broader product and customer data sources
Identify and close data collection gaps — if it can't be measured, help define how it should be
Design feedback loops that connect support signals to Product, Engineering, and Customer Success
Quantify the operational cost of product bugs, feature gaps, and onboarding failures
Contribute to QA analytics as the QA program matures
Track ticket deflection, AI/chatbot performance, and self-service effectiveness
Measure the impact of AI-driven support — containment rate, escalation rate from AI interactions, resolution quality — and surface findings that drive how AI tools are tuned and invested in
Support ad hoc analytical requests from the Support Operations Manager, User Operations leadership, and senior stakeholders

Required Qualifications

3–5 years of experience in analytics, with at least 2 years directly in support operations, customer success operations, or a closely adjacent function
Fluency in support platform data — understanding how Zendesk (or equivalent) is structured, what data it produces, and what it doesn't
SQL proficiency — ability to write complex queries against large datasets without hand-holding (CTEs, window functions, joins across schemas)
Dashboard experience — experience building and maintaining operational dashboards in Looker, Tableau, Sigma, Omni, or equivalent
Reporting for multiple audiences — understanding the difference between what a frontline manager needs and what a CFO needs, and building accordingly
Strong data storytelling — ability to not just present numbers, but write the narrative
Comfort operating solo — ability to deliver without a team around and without a ticket to dictate focus

Strong Plus

Experience with Python for data manipulation or automation
Familiarity with dbt or similar data transformation tooling
Experience building or contributing to QA analytics programs
Background supporting enterprise SaaS or AI-native products
Experience working with Zendesk APIs or extracting data beyond standard reporting

Key Attributes

AI-native: actively use AI tooling in analytical workflows — not as a novelty, but as a force multiplier
Pace: move in hours and days, not weeks. Surface findings before anyone has to ask
Judgment: know which metrics matter and which are vanity. Push back when framing is wrong
Clarity: outputs are direct, jargon-free, and actionable. Write for the reader, not yourself
Ownership: treat User Operations analytics as your problem to solve, not a ticket queue to process

Compensation

$112,000 - $168,000 USD

The hiring organization is an equal opportunity employer and does not discriminate on the basis of race, gender, sexual orientation, gender identity/expression, national origin, disability, age, genetic information, veteran status, marital status, pregnancy or related condition, or any other basis protected by law.
The organization is committed to providing reasonable accommodations to applicants with disabilities.

Additionally, this role is eligible to participate in the equity plan and benefits program. Benefits include, but not limited to: Comprehensive health, dental and vision coverage, retirement benefits (401k match up to 4%), and flexible PTO.

jobs.title: Research Project Coordinator
jobs.description:
Location:

Henry Hood Center For Health Research

Shift

Days (United States of America)

Scheduled Weekly Hours

40

Worker Type

Regular

Exemption Status

No

Job Summary

Under minimal supervision of an experienced Project Manager or designee, responsible for coordination of all aspects of research projects. Oversees day-to-day project activities such as subject recruitment, informed consent discussions, data collection and entry, and subject reimbursement processing and tracking.

Job Duties

Coordinates all research project-related activities, including recruitment, progress tracking and reporting, quality control, and research support staff training; oversees implementation of project plans.
Oversees development of study specific material such as tracking logs, templates, monitoring tools, study binders, process manuals, etc.
Submits request for data extracts and manages data collection, entry, and quality.
Tracks project related expenses and assists with monitoring study budgets, developing new study budgets and contract request submissions for noncomplex studies.
Prepares and submits IRB submissions of increasing complexity; reviews routine submissions prepared by Senior Research Assistants.
Handles sensitive project issues and provides resolution.
Ensures adherence to federal or other regulatory requirements.
Communicates resource needs, issues, project status and milestones to project team and project leader and devises corrective action plans.
Assists with preparation and submission of study manuscripts for publication, funding applications, and grant or sponsor-required reports.
Orientation, mentoring, and training of junior research staff.

Work is typically performed in an office environment. Accountable for satisfying all job specific obligations and complying with all organization policies and procedures. The specific statements in this profile are not intended to be all-inclusive. They represent typical elements considered necessary to successfully perform the job. *Relevant experience may be a combination of related work experience and/or Master's degree obtained (Master's Degree = 2 years relevant experience).

Education

Position Details:

Bachelor's Degree- (Required)

Experience

Minimum of 2 years-Relevant experience* (Required)

Skills

Certification(s) and License(s):

Communication, Computer Literacy, Multitasking, Organizing, Preceptor Skills, Teamwork

OUR PURPOSE & VALUES:
Everything we do is about caring for our patients, our members, our students, our Geisinger family and our communities.

KINDNESS: We strive to treat everyone as we would hope to be treated ourselves.
EXCELLENCE: We treasure colleagues who humbly strive for excellence.
LEARNING: We share our knowledge with the best and brightest to better prepare the caregivers for tomorrow.
INNOVATION: We constantly seek new and better ways to care for our patients, our members, our community, and the nation.
SAFETY: We provide a safe environment for our patients and members and the Geisinger family.

We offer healthcare benefits for full time and part time positions from day one, including vision, dental and domestic partners. Perhaps just as important, we encourage an atmosphere of collaboration, cooperation and collegiality.

We know that a diverse workforce with unique experiences and backgrounds makes our team stronger. Our patients, members and community come from a wide variety of backgrounds, and it takes a diverse workforce to make better health easier for all. We are proud to be an affirmative action, equal opportunity employer and all qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment regardless to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, disability or status as a protected veteran.

new_jobs.title: Junior Data Analyst
new_jobs.description:
Junior Data Analyst – Role Description & Qualifications
Role Overview
The Junior Data Analyst supports data-driven decision-making by collecting, processing, and analyzing data to generate meaningful insights. This role is designed for early-career professionals who are eager to develop analytical skills, work with structured datasets, and collaborate with cross-functional teams to support business objectives. The position offers hands-on experience with data analysis tools, reporting, and performance measurement.
Key Responsibilities
• Collect, clean, and validate data from multiple sources to ensure accuracy and consistency.
• Assist in analyzing datasets to identify trends, patterns, and performance indicators.
• Prepare regular and ad hoc reports, dashboards, and data summaries for internal stakeholders.
• Support data visualization efforts to present insights in a clear and actionable manner.
• Assist in maintaining databases and ensuring proper data documentation.
• Work closely with senior analysts to understand business requirements and analytical methodologies.
• Participate in quality checks and data governance activities.
• Help automate routine data processing and reporting tasks where applicable.
• Support performance tracking by monitoring key metrics and indicators.
• Contribute to continuous improvement of data analysis processes and tools.
Qualifications and Requirements
• Bachelor’s degree in Data Analytics, Statistics, Mathematics, Computer Science, Economics, or a related field.
• Basic understanding of data analysis concepts, statistical methods, and data structures.
• Familiarity with spreadsheets, databases, and data visualization tools.
• Introductory knowledge of query languages or analytical scripting is an advantage.
• Strong attention to detail and commitment to data accuracy.
• Analytical mindset with problem-solving capabilities.
• Ability to communicate findings clearly to both technical and non-technical audiences.
• Strong organizational skills and ability to manage multiple tasks.
• Willingness to learn new tools, techniques, and analytical frameworks.
• Experience from academic projects, internships, or personal data analysis work is a plus.
Summary
The Junior Data Analyst plays an important role in transforming raw data into valuable insights that support operational and strategic decisions. This position provides a solid foundation for building a career in data analytics through practical experience, collaboration, and continuous learning.

new_jobs.title: Junior Data Analyst
new_jobs.description:
Role Description
This role is for a Junior Data Analyst responsible for supporting data analysis, reporting, and insight generation that contribute to informed business decisions. In this position, you will work with data under guidance, helping transform raw information into structured insights that support operational and strategic objectives.
As a Junior Data Analyst, you will assist with collecting, cleaning, and organizing data from various sources to ensure accuracy and consistency. You will support routine analysis, help maintain datasets, and contribute to the preparation of reports and dashboards used by internal teams.
Key responsibilities include analyzing basic trends and performance metrics, identifying data inconsistencies, and supporting ongoing analytical projects. You will help document methodologies, assumptions, and findings to ensure transparency and repeatability.
You will collaborate with analysts, managers, and stakeholders to understand data requirements and support analytical workflows. Your role will involve responding to data requests, maintaining reporting schedules, and assisting with continuous improvements in data quality and processes.
This position requires a strong interest in data analysis, attention to detail, and a structured approach to problem-solving. You will be expected to follow best practices, validate results, and continuously develop analytical skills.
Clear communication is essential, as you will help explain data findings in a simple and structured manner. By supporting accurate analysis and reliable reporting, the Junior Data Analyst plays an important role in enabling data-driven decision-making across the organization.
Key Responsibilities
Collect, clean, and organize data from multiple sources
Support routine data analysis and reporting activities
Assist in preparing dashboards, reports, and summaries
Identify data quality issues and support data validation
Maintain organized datasets and documentation
Support performance tracking and metric analysis
Respond to data requests from internal teams
Document analysis methods and assumptions
Follow data standards and best practices
Assist with continuous improvement of data processes

Qualifications
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills
High attention to detail and data accuracy
Ability to work with spreadsheets and basic analysis tools
Interest in data analysis and reporting
Ability to organize and interpret information clearly
Clear written and verbal communication skills
Ability to manage tasks and deadlines effectively
Willingness to learn and develop analytical skills
Structured and reliable working style

new_jobs.title: Data Analyst
new_jobs.description:
Data Analyst – Role Description & Qualifications
Role Overview
The Data Analyst is responsible for transforming raw data into meaningful insights that support business decisions and operational efficiency. This role focuses on data collection, analysis, reporting, and visualization to identify trends, measure performance, and uncover opportunities for improvement. The Data Analyst works closely with stakeholders across departments to ensure data-driven strategies align with organizational goals.
Key Responsibilities
• Collect, process, and analyze data from multiple internal and external sources.
• Validate, clean, and structure datasets to ensure accuracy and consistency.
• Develop reports, dashboards, and visualizations to communicate insights effectively.
• Analyze trends, patterns, and key performance indicators to support decision-making.
• Collaborate with business teams to understand analytical requirements and objectives.
• Support ad hoc analysis requests and present findings to stakeholders.
• Assist in defining data standards, metrics, and documentation practices.
• Identify data quality issues and recommend corrective actions.
• Contribute to automation and optimization of data analysis and reporting processes.
• Stay informed about analytical tools, techniques, and best practices.
Qualifications and Requirements
• Bachelor’s degree in Data Analytics, Statistics, Mathematics, Computer Science, Economics, or a related field.
• Strong understanding of data analysis techniques, statistical concepts, and data structures.
• Experience working with datasets, databases, and reporting tools.
• Proficiency in spreadsheets and data visualization platforms; familiarity with query languages or analytical scripting is a plus.
• Strong analytical thinking and problem-solving skills.
• High attention to detail and commitment to data accuracy.
• Ability to translate complex data into clear, actionable insights.
• Strong communication and collaboration skills.
• Ability to manage multiple projects and meet deadlines effectively.
• Experience gained through professional roles, academic projects, or internships is advantageous.
Summary
The Data Analyst plays a vital role in enabling data-driven decision-making by delivering accurate analysis and actionable insights. This position offers opportunities to work with diverse datasets, collaborate across teams, and contribute directly to strategic and operational improvements within the organization.

new_jobs.title: Physics Data Analyst - AI Trainer
new_jobs.description:
We are looking for an existing Physics Data Analyst to help train AI models. You will measure the progress of these AI chatbots, evaluate their logic, and solve problems to improve the quality of each model.

In this role, you will need to hold an expert level of physics. Other related fields include, but are not limited to: Physics and/or Applied Mathematics.

Advantages Of Contracting With Us

You'll be able to choose which projects you want to work on and when
You work on your own schedule, on your own computer, from the comfort of your own home
Projects are paid hourly starting at $40+ USD per hour, with bonus rates available on some projects

Responsibilities

Give AI chatbots complex physics problems and evaluate their outputs
Evaluate the quality produced by AI models for correctness and performance

Qualifications

Fluency in English (native or bilingual level)
Detail-oriented
Strong grasp of classical mechanics, E&M, thermodynamics, and basic quantum concepts
A current, in progress, or completed Master's and/or PhD is preferred but not required

Note

Payment is made via PayPal. We will never ask for any money from you. PayPal will handle any currency conversions from USD. Only applicants in the United States will be considered for this role. This is an independent contractor position.

new_jobs.title: Senior Data Analyst
new_jobs.description:
U.S. News & World Report is a multifaceted digital media company dedicated to helping citizens, consumers, business leaders and policy officials make important decisions in their lives. We publish independent reporting, rankings, data journalism and advice that has earned the trust of our readers and users for nearly 90 years. Our platforms on usnews.com include Education, Health, Money, Travel, Cars, News and 360 Reviews.

We reach millions of people monthly during moments when they are most in need of expert advice and motivated to act on that advice directly on our platforms. Our signature franchises include our “Best” series of consumer guides on colleges, graduate schools, hospitals, diets, cars, financial services and more. These guides provide an easy-to-digest list for consumers to better understand and compare when making their decisions. We continue to publish annual guides of the authoritative Best Colleges and Best Hospitals rankings on our website and in print. And our U.S. News Live flagship conferences highlight important national conversations including Healthcare of Tomorrow.

Your role in helping us shape the future:

U.S. News empowers everyone to thrive. As a data analyst on our Money team, you will collaborate closely with a cross-functional team to guide the development of products and services from conception to launch, and engage closely with our partnerships and editorial teams to help maximize success on both fronts.

The primary responsibilities of the Data Analyst are:

Providing tactical support to product managers through the entire development lifecycle, including assisting with technical specifications and QA.
Analyzing key metrics to understand product performance, including running A/B tests, predictive modeling, and dashboard development
Generate revenue yield analysis and recommendations on partner campaigns/budgets (including both paid media and on page placements across the US News experience)
Access and manipulate large external datasets to enable our editorial experts to publish unique data driven content pieces and advice

Are you up to the challenge?

Proven ability to quickly adapt to a rapidly changing environment while maintaining quality and adhering to deadlines
Develop and maintain positive working relationships stakeholders across multiple functions and teams
Effectively share and communicate data insights and analysis across different parts of the organization from engineers to executives

You should definitely have:

Bachelor’s degree in statistics, data science, economics, computer science, or any other relevant fields
Minimum 5 years’ experience coding with statistical software packages, preferably STATA
Expert level proficiency in SQL, R, SAS, Python or other data science programming languages
Ability to work with large datasets and summarize key insights
Data mining, analysis, and presentation skills
Proficiency using Microsoft Excel or Google Sheets to summarize and present data in a concise and easy to understand manner
Strong research skills and the ability to answer questions with internal and external data sources
Experience presenting analyses to non-technical audiences with simple explanations
Attention to detail, organization and time management are your strengths

It would be nice if you have:

Experience with reporting/tracking tools including Google Analytics
Up-to-date knowledge on Internet publishing and metrics, with an acute understanding of SEO

What it’s like to work with us:

Talent is our best asset!

We invest in people with passion and potential who understand U.S. News’ dedication to our consumers.
Entrepreneurial, mission-driven culture with core values of quality and integrity
Focus on fostering personal and professional growth
Competitive benefits including paid vacation time, medical, tuition reimbursement, and training
Collaborative Work Environment : Fun, diverse, inclusive and ambitious co-workers

Other Job Info:

These statements are intended to describe the general nature and level of work being performed by employees assigned to this job. This is not intended to be an exhaustive list of all responsibilities, duties, and skills required of employees assigned to this job.
U.S. News & World Report strongly encourages all employees to be fully vaccinated (including boosters).
Preference for New York or Washington, DC based applicants to work a hybrid schedule in those office locations. Remote applicants will be considered.
The anticipated base salary for this position is $65,000-$85,000. The actual base salary offered will depend on a variety of factors, including without limitation, the qualifications of the individual applicant for the position, years of relevant experience, level of education attained, certifications or other professional licenses held, interview performance, and if applicable, the location in which the applicant lives and/or from which they will be performing the job.

new_jobs.title: Staff Environmental Scientist - NPDES Permitting (Multiple Locations)
new_jobs.description:
Description

The Staff Environmental Scientist will support NPDES permitting, water quality studies, environmental assessments, and compliance efforts for various types of projects including petrochemical plants, power generation, renewable fuels, industrial sites, and water treatment facilities. This position supports the growth of the Environmental Services Global Practice based upon Burns & McDonnell's core values of safety, client satisfaction, commitment, dedication, integrity, and overall outstanding service.

Assigned Tasks May Include The Following

Apply environmental regulatory knowledge and experience to develop effective strategies and solutions to current and future technical and regulatory issues on projects.
Evaluate wastewater and stormwater discharges associated with industrial projects in the early stages of development and provide permitting approach and operational compliance strategies.
Evaluate potential sites for environmental issues that may be critical to project development.
Support development of NPDES permit applications and associated studies including reasonable potential analysis, antidegradation analysis, 316(b) assessments, ELG applicability reviews, mixing zone analysis, and water quality modeling (e.g., CORMIX, QUAL2K, WASP). 
Monitor state and federal regulatory developments (proposed and promulgated rule making, notices, etc.).
Prepare operational and construction Stormwater Pollution Prevention Plans (SWPPP) and SPCC Plans and conduct compliance inspections and audits.
Establish, build, and maintain relationships with internal Burns & McDonnell customers (in the engineering practices) and external clients.
All other duties as assigned.

Qualifications

Bachelor's degree in Environmental Science, Chemistry, Toxicology, or related field from an accredited program is required.
Minimum of 4 years of related professional experience (5 years preferred). 
Prior consulting experience preferred.
Knowledge of federal and state regulations relating to water quality, e.g., Section 402 of the Clean Water Act.
Experience preparing NPDES permit applications and performing reasonable potential analyses.
Experience with large data sets. Laboratory experience preferred.
Regulatory and technical familiarity with wastewater and stormwater systems.
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills.
Excellent written & verbal communication skills.
High level of attention to detail.
Ability to adjust to rapidly changing work environments and research approaches.
Must be proficient in the use of computer software (Microsoft Word, Excel, PowerPoint).
Valid driver's license required. In addition, must meet standards to qualify for and maintain the Company's vehicle driving privileges as outlined in the Company's Motor Vehicle Safety Policy. 

This job posting will remain open a minimum of 72 hours and on an ongoing basis until filled.

EEO/Disabled/Veterans

new_jobs.title: Project Coordinator - Life Science Construction
new_jobs.description:
From the inception of a project through to completion and beyond, Turner & Townsend help to deliver the outcomes that matter through transformational programs covering the full spectrum of consultancy, project delivery and post-project operations.

With offices located globally, you're never far away from our services. Working from 133 offices in 49 countries, we make the difference to projects across the real estate, infrastructure and natural resources sectors worldwide.

Our team is dynamic, innovative and client-focused, supported by an inclusive and fun company culture. Our clients value our proactive approach, depth of expertise, integrity and the quality we deliver. As a result, our people get to enjoy working on some of the most exciting projects in the world.

Job Description

Turner & Townsend
are engaged to support our client with many complex projects, and we are seeking an ambitious Project Coordinator
to join our prestigious client’s rapidly growing Life Sciences team. You have a chance to be a key player in our technological future, as part of a team of innovative professionals shaping our built environment.

The role requires a smart, motivated professional to work closely with global facilities teams and various internal departments including Finance, Procurement, Security and Legal to support all aspects of their rapidly expanding footprint.
Assist in monitoring and documenting project progress and other key performance indicators.
Assist in managing internal project budgets within appropriate software platforms for monthly/quarterly/yearly financial reporting.
Assist in compiling contractual documentation to support contract and PO issuances for assigned vendors.
Perform document control duties as required.
Issue and track purchase orders for new and existing vendors.
Manage monthly vendor invoice review process within invoicing software, including project management review, 3rd party auditing services, and communication with Accounts Payable.
Facilitate change order routing and review processes within project management software.
Assist in scheduling coordination meetings and job walks.
Assist in financial closeout of internal budgets, Vendor POs, etc.

Qualifications

BA/BS or equivalent in financial management or controls. Relevant work experience will also be considered in lieu of degree.
Minimum of (3) year’s professional experience as project coordinator or administration.
Strong collaboration and relationship building skills.
Excellent communication skills and presentation skills.
A problem-solving mindset.
Expert in Microsoft and Google Suite of software products; especially Excel.

Additional Information

*On-site presence and requirements may change depending on our client's needs*

Our inspired people share our vision and mission. We provide a great place to work, where each person has the opportunity and voice to affect change.

We want our people to succeed both in work and life. To support this we promote a healthy, productive and flexible working environment that respects work-life balance.

Turner & Townsend is an equal opportunity employer. We celebrate diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees and actively encourage applications from all sectors of the community.

Please find out more about us at www.turnerandtownsend.com/ and https://www.heery.com/

All your information will be kept confidential according to EEO guidelines.

Join our social media conversations for more information about Turner & Townsend and our exciting future projects:

Twitter

Instagram

LinkedIn

It is strictly against Turner & Townsend policy for candidates to pay any fee in relation to our recruitment process. No recruitment agency working with Turner & Townsend will ask candidates to pay a fee at any time.

Any unsolicited resumes/CVs submitted through our website or to Turner & Townsend personal e-mail accounts, are considered property of Turner & Townsend and are not subject to payment of agency fees. In order to be an authorised Recruitment Agency/Search Firm for Turner & Townsend, there must be a formal written agreement in place and the agency must be invited, by the Recruitment Team, to submit candidates for review.

new_jobs.title: Data Analyst
new_jobs.description:
About The Company

Ryder System, Inc. is a leading provider of transportation and supply chain management solutions. With decades of experience, Ryder has established itself as a trusted partner for organizations seeking innovative logistics, fleet management, and dedicated transportation services. Committed to operational excellence and customer satisfaction, Ryder leverages advanced technology and industry expertise to deliver tailored solutions that drive efficiency and growth for its clients worldwide. The company's dedication to sustainability, safety, and continuous improvement underscores its position as a forward-thinking leader in the logistics industry.

About The Role

We are seeking a highly motivated and detail-oriented professional to join Ryder System, Inc. as a Supply Chain Analyst. In this role, you will be responsible for analyzing supply chain processes, identifying areas for improvement, and implementing strategic initiatives to optimize logistics operations. The ideal candidate will possess strong analytical skills, a deep understanding of supply chain management principles, and the ability to collaborate effectively with cross-functional teams. This position offers an excellent opportunity to contribute to the company's mission of delivering innovative solutions that enhance operational efficiency and customer satisfaction.

Qualifications

The ideal candidate should have a bachelor’s degree in Supply Chain Management, Logistics, Business Administration, or a related field. Proven experience of at least 3 years in supply chain analysis or logistics operations is preferred. Strong proficiency in data analysis tools such as Excel, SAP, or other ERP systems is essential. Excellent communication and problem-solving skills are required to effectively collaborate with team members and stakeholders. A solid understanding of transportation, warehousing, inventory management, and procurement processes is highly desirable. The ability to work under pressure and manage multiple priorities in a fast-paced environment is also important.

Responsibilities

Analyze supply chain data to identify inefficiencies and develop actionable insights for process improvement. Collaborate with logistics, procurement, and warehouse teams to streamline operations and reduce costs. Monitor key performance indicators (KPIs) and prepare detailed reports to track progress and inform decision-making. Assist in the development and implementation of supply chain strategies aligned with company objectives. Support the integration of new technologies and systems to enhance supply chain visibility and automation. Maintain accurate records of supply chain activities and ensure compliance with company policies and industry regulations. Participate in cross-functional projects aimed at optimizing transportation routes, inventory levels, and supplier relationships. Provide training and support to team members on best practices and new processes.

Benefits

Ryder offers a comprehensive benefits package that includes competitive salary, health insurance, dental and vision coverage, and retirement plans. Employees are also eligible for performance-based bonuses and incentives. The company promotes a healthy work-life balance through flexible work arrangements and paid time off. Additional benefits include professional development opportunities, tuition reimbursement, and access to wellness programs. Ryder’s commitment to employee growth and well-being makes it an ideal place for professionals seeking a rewarding career in supply chain management.

Equal Opportunity

Ryder System, Inc. is an equal opportunity employer. We celebrate diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees. We do not discriminate based on race, color, religion, gender, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, age, disability, or any other protected characteristic. We believe that a diverse workforce enhances our ability to serve our clients and innovate in the logistics industry, and we encourage individuals from all backgrounds to apply.

new_jobs.title: Health Data Analyst - AI Trainer
new_jobs.description:
About The Job

We are looking for existing quantitative professionals to help train AI models. In this role, you will work closely with state-of-the-art AI models on tasks like evaluating AI-generated quantitative analyses, solving technical problems, and providing feedback that directly shapes how these systems reason about data, models, and scientific problems. Whether your background is in data science, astrophysics, economics, biostatistics, operations research, or any other quantitative field, if you think rigorously about data and models, your skills are directly applicable here. Some team members fit this work alongside a full-time role, while others treat it as their primary focus.

To get started, once you sign up for an account, you'll take a short assessment (this serves as our version of an interview). If you pass, you'll receive an email confirmation, and paid work will become available on our platform.

Advantages Of Contracting With Us

You'll be able to choose which projects you want to work on and when
You work on your own schedule, on your own computer, from the comfort of your own home
Projects are paid hourly starting at $50-$100+ USD per hour, with bonus rates available on some projects

Responsibilities

Evaluate AI-generated quantitative work, including statistical analysis, predictive modeling, scientific reasoning, and data-driven insights, for technical accuracy and real-world validity.
Design and solve quantitative problems used to train and benchmark AI systems, spanning areas like forecasting, experimental analysis, optimization, and statistical inference.
Write clear technical explanations and well-documented analytical code.
Provide feedback that directly shapes the next generation of AI models built for quantitative reasoning.

Qualifications

2+ years of hands-on experience in a quantitative role or research environment — such as data science, statistics, economics, finance, physics, biology, epidemiology, operations research, or any adjacent field.
Some coding experience required, with comfort writing and reviewing analytical code end-to-end.
Practical experience with statistical methods, predictive modeling, and experiment design (e.g., A/B testing, hypothesis testing, regression, classification, time-series forecasting).
Fluency in English (native or bilingual level) with strong writing skills.
A bachelor's degree in a quantitative field is preferred (Statistics, Computer Science, Mathematics, Engineering, or similar); a master's or PhD is a plus.
Relevant credentials are a plus (e.g., Kaggle Competition ranking, AWS/GCP ML certifications, or equivalent demonstrated expertise).

Note: Payment is made via PayPal. We will never ask for any money from you. PayPal will handle any currency conversions from USD. Only applicants in the United States, Canada, United Kingdom, Ireland, Australia, and New Zealand will be considered for this role. This is an independent contractor position.

new_jobs.title: Support Operations Data Analyst
new_jobs.description:
Swooped is a Talent Platform (not a staffing agency). We are not the employer for this role and do not make hiring decisions. Clicking "Apply" takes you to Swooped to review the role and hiring company details.

About the Opportunity

The organization is transforming how legal and professional services operate. By combining frontier agentic AI, an enterprise-grade platform, and deep domain expertise, the company is reshaping how critical knowledge work gets done for decades to come.

This is a rare chance to help build a generational company at a true inflection point. With a significant customer base in numerous countries, strong product-market fit, and world-class investor support, the organization is scaling fast and defining a new category in real time. The work is ambitious, the bar is high, and the opportunity for growth — personal, professional, and financial — is unmatched.

The team moves fast, takes ownership, and is deeply committed to the mission — operating with intensity, staying close to customers, and pushing each other for excellence. The organization lives by three values: Decisiveness, Simplicity, and Job's Not Finished. Actions are taken quickly on clear judgment over perfect information, simplicity is believed to be what scales, and there is never satisfaction with the current state. If you want to do the best work of your career alongside people who share that drive, the organization would love to build with you.

The future of professional services is being written today — and the organization is just getting started.

Role Overview

User Operations runs on data — but right now, that data lives in too many places, speaks too many languages, and reaches the wrong people too late. This role exists to fix that.

As the first Support Operations Data Analyst
, this role will own the analytics function for the User Operations organization. The individual will build and maintain the dashboards, reports, and feedback loops that indicate whether the organization is hitting its north stars — cSAT, TTR, QA scores, escalation rates — and surface the signal underneath the numbers so action can be taken. This role will sit within the Support Operations team, reporting to the Support Operations Manager, and work closely with User Operations leadership and the central data team to ensure the organization is equipped with the right instrumentation as it scales.

This is a solo role. There will not be a team beneath the individual. The candidate will need to be fluent enough in support analytics to hold the function independently, confident enough to push back on how metrics are framed, and fast enough to operate at the organization's pace.

Key Responsibilities

Own recurring reporting for User Operations — weekly, monthly, and QBR-ready — tailored to ops, leadership, and cross-functional audiences
Translate support data into clear narratives: what's happening, why, and what to do about it
Track and maintain north star metrics: cSAT, TTR by tier, QA scores, bug escalation rate to EPD, and First Response Time
Build and maintain self-serve dashboards that give the ops team and leadership real-time visibility into support performance
Partner with Support Systems to ensure Zendesk is instrumented to capture the data needed
Work with the central data team to connect support data to broader product and customer data sources
Identify and close data collection gaps — if it can't be measured, help define how it should be
Design feedback loops that connect support signals to Product, Engineering, and Customer Success
Quantify the operational cost of product bugs, feature gaps, and onboarding failures
Contribute to QA analytics as the QA program matures
Track ticket deflection, AI/chatbot performance, and self-service effectiveness
Measure the impact of AI-driven support — containment rate, escalation rate from AI interactions, resolution quality — and surface findings that drive how AI tools are tuned and invested in
Support ad hoc analytical requests from the Support Operations Manager, User Operations leadership, and senior stakeholders

Required Qualifications

3–5 years of experience in analytics, with at least 2 years directly in support operations, customer success operations, or a closely adjacent function
Fluency in support platform data — understanding how Zendesk (or equivalent) is structured, what data it produces, and what it doesn't
SQL proficiency — ability to write complex queries against large datasets without hand-holding (CTEs, window functions, joins across schemas)
Dashboard experience — experience building and maintaining operational dashboards in Looker, Tableau, Sigma, Omni, or equivalent
Reporting for multiple audiences — understanding the difference between what a frontline manager needs and what a CFO needs, and building accordingly
Strong data storytelling — ability to not just present numbers, but write the narrative
Comfort operating solo — ability to deliver without a team around and without a ticket to dictate focus

Strong Plus

Experience with Python for data manipulation or automation
Familiarity with dbt or similar data transformation tooling
Experience building or contributing to QA analytics programs
Background supporting enterprise SaaS or AI-native products
Experience working with Zendesk APIs or extracting data beyond standard reporting

Key Attributes

AI-native: actively use AI tooling in analytical workflows — not as a novelty, but as a force multiplier
Pace: move in hours and days, not weeks. Surface findings before anyone has to ask
Judgment: know which metrics matter and which are vanity. Push back when framing is wrong
Clarity: outputs are direct, jargon-free, and actionable. Write for the reader, not yourself
Ownership: treat User Operations analytics as your problem to solve, not a ticket queue to process

Compensation

$112,000 - $168,000 USD

The hiring organization is an equal opportunity employer and does not discriminate on the basis of race, gender, sexual orientation, gender identity/expression, national origin, disability, age, genetic information, veteran status, marital status, pregnancy or related condition, or any other basis protected by law.
The organization is committed to providing reasonable accommodations to applicants with disabilities.

Additionally, this role is eligible to participate in the equity plan and benefits program. Benefits include, but not limited to: Comprehensive health, dental and vision coverage, retirement benefits (401k match up to 4%), and flexible PTO.

new_jobs.title: Research Project Coordinator
new_jobs.description:
Location:

Henry Hood Center For Health Research

Shift

Days (United States of America)

Scheduled Weekly Hours

40

Worker Type

Regular

Exemption Status

No

Job Summary

Under minimal supervision of an experienced Project Manager or designee, responsible for coordination of all aspects of research projects. Oversees day-to-day project activities such as subject recruitment, informed consent discussions, data collection and entry, and subject reimbursement processing and tracking.

Job Duties

Coordinates all research project-related activities, including recruitment, progress tracking and reporting, quality control, and research support staff training; oversees implementation of project plans.
Oversees development of study specific material such as tracking logs, templates, monitoring tools, study binders, process manuals, etc.
Submits request for data extracts and manages data collection, entry, and quality.
Tracks project related expenses and assists with monitoring study budgets, developing new study budgets and contract request submissions for noncomplex studies.
Prepares and submits IRB submissions of increasing complexity; reviews routine submissions prepared by Senior Research Assistants.
Handles sensitive project issues and provides resolution.
Ensures adherence to federal or other regulatory requirements.
Communicates resource needs, issues, project status and milestones to project team and project leader and devises corrective action plans.
Assists with preparation and submission of study manuscripts for publication, funding applications, and grant or sponsor-required reports.
Orientation, mentoring, and training of junior research staff.

Work is typically performed in an office environment. Accountable for satisfying all job specific obligations and complying with all organization policies and procedures. The specific statements in this profile are not intended to be all-inclusive. They represent typical elements considered necessary to successfully perform the job. *Relevant experience may be a combination of related work experience and/or Master's degree obtained (Master's Degree = 2 years relevant experience).

Education

Position Details:

Bachelor's Degree- (Required)

Experience

Minimum of 2 years-Relevant experience* (Required)

Skills

Certification(s) and License(s):

Communication, Computer Literacy, Multitasking, Organizing, Preceptor Skills, Teamwork

OUR PURPOSE & VALUES:
Everything we do is about caring for our patients, our members, our students, our Geisinger family and our communities.

KINDNESS: We strive to treat everyone as we would hope to be treated ourselves.
EXCELLENCE: We treasure colleagues who humbly strive for excellence.
LEARNING: We share our knowledge with the best and brightest to better prepare the caregivers for tomorrow.
INNOVATION: We constantly seek new and better ways to care for our patients, our members, our community, and the nation.
SAFETY: We provide a safe environment for our patients and members and the Geisinger family.

We offer healthcare benefits for full time and part time positions from day one, including vision, dental and domestic partners. Perhaps just as important, we encourage an atmosphere of collaboration, cooperation and collegiality.

We know that a diverse workforce with unique experiences and backgrounds makes our team stronger. Our patients, members and community come from a wide variety of backgrounds, and it takes a diverse workforce to make better health easier for all. We are proud to be an affirmative action, equal opportunity employer and all qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment regardless to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, disability or status as a protected veteran.
